_note: Held-out cases for Job 6 (one-line result-summary cards / Hex Magic). `recordedResponse` is a representative raw model output for --dry-run. Scoring = keyword coverage + length cap + non-empty (or empty when the parser is expected to drop the response for hallucinating a column).

input.sql: SELECT vendor_name, SUM(amount) AS total FROM invoices GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY total DESC LIMIT 5

cases.recordedResponse: {"observation":"Top vendor by spend is Acme Corp at 12,340.50 on `total`, ahead of Globex at 9,821.10 — top 5 captures most of the long tail."}

input.sql: SELECT payment_mode, COUNT(*) AS n FROM payments GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY n DESC

cases.recordedResponse: {"observation":"UPI dominates with 1,240 of 2,425 payments — more than the next two `payment_mode` values combined."}

input.sql: SELECT vendor_name, SUM(amount) AS total FROM invoices GROUP BY 1

cases.recordedResponse: {"observation":"`country` is the dominant grouping with `A` at 100."}

input.sql: SELECT iso_date, COUNT(*) AS n FROM events GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY iso_date

cases.recordedResponse: {"observation":"Daily events range from 55 to 201 over the sampled 30 day window, with a noticeable peak around 2026-04-04; the rest of the window stays in the high two-digit to low three-digit band, suggesting steady traffic with one outlier worth a closer look."}

input.sql: SELECT log_level, COUNT(*) AS n FROM access_log GROUP BY 1
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) AS unique_user_id FROM events

cases.recordedResponse: {"observation":"18,432 unique users in the result; `unique_user_id` is a single-row stat."}
